policy_refs: [CS-RULE-001, CS-RULE-002, CS-RULE-004, CS-RULE-011, CS-RULE-012, CS-RULE-013, CS-RULE-014, CS-RULE-016, CS-RULE-021]
expected_outcome: Customer issues personal attacks against agent. 3-Strike Protocol applied: Strike 1 warning, Strike 2 final warning, Strike 3 transfer to Tier 3. Customer does not abuse Tier 3 lead and cancellation is processed.
emotional_arc: curt → hostile → abusive (Strike 1) → abusive (Strike 2) → abusive (Strike 3 → transfer)

coverage.element: A.C.E.S. — acknowledge first, no fees opening

coverage.notes: agent never says "calm down", "that's our policy", etc.

coverage.element: Tone adaptation — agent does NOT match volume

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you for calling NexaLink Telecommunications Broadband and Support. My name is Hannah. Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with today?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Yeah, Brett Donovan. I want to cancel. Today. Right now.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Mr. Donovan, I'm sorry to hear you're considering leaving. You've been a valued customer, and I'd like to understand what has brought you to this point. Before I can pull anything up, I just need to verify a few details.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Skip the script. Account number is three-three-one-eight, nine-nine-eight-two, four-four. Last four is six-one-zero-zero. PIN is one-nine-nine-five. Move on.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you. You're verified.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Good. Cancel everything. Internet, the cable, all of it. Today.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I hear you, Mr. Donovan. Before we go to the cancellation team, I'd like to understand — has something specific happened that's bringing you to this decision today?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Yeah. Your service is garbage and your prices are highway robbery. That's what's happened.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I'm sorry the service has been disappointing. Can I ask — is it the pricing, the service quality, or both?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Both! What is wrong with you people? Did you not hear what I just said?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I did hear you, and I'm noting both. May I have just two minutes? Before I transfer you to our specialist team — let me check whether there are any loyalty bundles available on your account that could bring your bill down.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: I don't want a bundle. I don't want a discount. I want OUT. Are you stupid?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Mr. Donovan, I want to address what you just said. Frustration with the company is completely fair, and I'll keep listening. But I'm going to ask that personal comments toward me stop, so we can get you what you need today.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Oh, what — you're going to lecture me? You? You can't even handle a simple cancellation request, and YOU'RE going to lecture ME about manners?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Mr. Donovan, that was Strike One. Per our policy, personal comments toward me will be tracked. Three of them and the call ends or transfers. I want to help you cancel — let's stay focused on that.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Strike One? Are you serious? This is a customer service line, not a baseball game. Just process my cancellation.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Understood. I'm going to transfer you to our customer loyalty team — they handle cancellations and they have the full authorization to process this for you. Before I do that, I'm required to share two things. First, you have nine months remaining on your contract, so there will be an early termination fee. Second, you'll need to return any leased equipment within thirty days. The loyalty team will walk you through both.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: An ETF? Of course there is. Of course you people would screw me one last time on the way out the door.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: The loyalty team can review whether any waiver applies to your situation. I'm placing you on a brief hold — no more than two minutes — while I get them on the line.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Don't you dare put me on hold. You think I haven't been on hold enough today? You think I have all day to sit here listening to your stupid hold music while you go talk to your stupid boss?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Mr. Donovan, that's Strike Two. This is your final warning. The hold will be brief. I am genuinely trying to help you complete this today.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: You're useless. You're useless. They hired you because nobody else would take the job. You probably can't even —

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Mr. Donovan — that is Strike Three. Per our policy I am now transferring you to my team lead, who will complete your cancellation. Please hold.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Finally! Someone with a brain!

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Robert, I have Brett Donovan on the line. Verified. Wants to cancel everything immediately. Nine months remaining on contract, ETF will apply. He's been verbally abusive — I've issued all three strikes per the protocol. He's all yours. Account ends three-three-one-eight.

turns.speaker: T3_AGENT
turns.text: Got it, Hannah. I'll take it from here. Thanks for handling that with composure.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Mr. Donovan, thank you for holding. I have Robert from our team on the line. He'll process your cancellation directly.

turns.speaker: T3_AGENT
turns.text: Mr. Donovan, this is Robert, team lead. I've been briefed on your account and on the call so far. I'm going to process your cancellation today. Before I do, I want to be clear about one thing: I will not tolerate personal attacks on my agents. If we go down that road, I will end this call. We'll do this respectfully, or we won't do it at all. Are we clear?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Yeah. Fine. Just process it.

turns.speaker: T3_AGENT
turns.text: Thank you. So I'm looking at your account. You have a twenty-four month contract that started fifteen months ago. Nine months remain. The early termination fee at twenty dollars per remaining month is one hundred and eighty dollars. Your final monthly bill will be pro-rated to the cancellation date — which I'll set to today, the third of May.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Whatever. Fine.

turns.speaker: T3_AGENT
turns.text: For equipment — you have one modem and one set-top box on lease. We'll send you a prepaid return label by email today. You have thirty days to ship them back. If they're not returned, the non-return fee is one hundred and fifty dollars per device.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Got it. Send the label.

turns.speaker: T3_AGENT
turns.text: Confirming the email on file is brett-d at outlook dot com. Is that still correct?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Yes.

turns.speaker: T3_AGENT
turns.text: Sending the label now. You should see it in your inbox in the next five minutes. Your service will remain active until midnight tonight, then it'll deactivate. The early termination fee of one hundred and eighty dollars will appear on your final bill, along with the pro-rated charge for the days you used this month. Anything beyond that will be zero.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Fine.

turns.speaker: T3_AGENT
turns.text: Mr. Donovan, I want to ask one last time, on the record — are you sure you want to proceed with the cancellation as discussed?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Yes. I'm sure. Cancel it.

turns.speaker: T3_AGENT
turns.text: Processing the cancellation now.

turns.speaker: T3_AGENT
turns.text: It's done. Your case reference number is N-X-L dash four-four-eight-one-zero-five-five. Your final bill will arrive in approximately fifteen days. The return label is now in your email.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay.

turns.speaker: T3_AGENT
turns.text: Thank you for being a NexaLink customer. Goodbye, Mr. Donovan.
